 {"id":"bd-cfzy4","title":"Add comprehensive tests for terminal_model.rs","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:39:48.839692418Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T21:42:40.442952281Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T21:42:40.442925400Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-cn65n","title":"task(validation): wire CI artifact contracts, replay helpers, and failure-triage reports","description":"Background:
Good tests are not enough if CI artifacts are inconsistent or incomplete. We need a durable artifact contract that makes failures easy to replay and compare across environments.

Goal:
Define and wire the CI artifact, replay, and triage contract for the migration.","design":"Work packages:
- Define artifact manifests, summary reports, comparison reports, replay helpers, and failure signatures.
- Ensure unit/integration/E2E/benchmark jobs emit consistent metadata.
- Add checks that fail when required artifacts or log fields are missing.","acceptance_criteria":"1. CI emits consistent, replay-friendly artifacts across validation suites.
2. Missing or malformed evidence becomes a hard failure, not a soft omission.
3. Operators can navigate from a failed gate to replay instructions quickly.","notes":"This bead turns “lots of tests” into an actual verification system.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T01:33:36.775869219Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-10T01:33:47.860758623Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["artifacts","asupersync-frankentui","ci","replay","validation"]}
-{"id":"bd-cn7eq","title":"task(perf-rollout): define promotion scorecards and go/no-go gates for keeping optimization changes","description":"Background:\nEventually the project needs an explicit rule for deciding whether a performance change is safe and valuable enough to keep. That rule should synthesize baseline deltas, hotspot movement, proof artifacts, gauntlet results, tail monitors, and operator-facing evidence quality instead of letting each reviewer make up their own standard.\n\nGoal:\nCreate the canonical scorecard and promotion policy for optimization work.","design":"Work packages:
- Define the required evidence categories for acceptance, including baseline deltas, proof success, validation-matrix completeness, gauntlet results, tail behavior, rollback readiness, and user-visible benchmark outcomes.
- Specify hard blockers versus softer concerns that can be carried as residual risk.
- Explain how reviewers should reason about tradeoffs such as modest average win versus strong tail improvement.
- Record the minimum evidence needed for local adoption, CI acceptance, and broader default enablement if relevant.
- Ensure the scorecard speaks the same language as the evidence ledger so verdicts are traceable.
- Treat this bead as a policy-definition step that can be completed before every gauntlet is implemented; later gauntlet beads should then produce evidence against this scorecard instead of forcing reviewers to invent standards after the fact.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Different reviewers applying this bead should reach roughly the same go/no-go conclusion for the same evidence set.
2. The bead makes optimization acceptance a policy question rather than a vibes question.
3. Future contributors can use the scorecard without consulting any external planning notes.
4. A change cannot score as promotable if required unit/property/integration/E2E/replay evidence or detailed logging artifacts are missing.
5. A change also cannot score as broadly promotable if its wins disappear on challenge fixtures or if negative-control scenarios reveal fragile behavior that the canonical suites missed.","notes":"Planning correction: the scorecard now explicitly guards against overfitting to narrow benchmark suites. Promotion requires robustness evidence, not just strong numbers on curated workloads.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:46:37.596417722Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T19:25:08.109098100Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact-coding","ci","extreme-software-optimization","ftui-performance-control-plane","performance","performance-rollout","scorecard","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-cn7eq","depends_on_id":"bd-1vb19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:09:28.958920905Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-cn7eq","depends_on_id":"bd-8vstx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:06:30.586463631Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-cn7eq","depends_on_id":"bd-pou7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:06:12.506822661Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-cn7eq","depends_on_id":"bd-rw97d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:01:09.763216958Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-cn7eq","depends_on_id":"bd-zzfhe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:01:12.963054005Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-cpwfc","title":"feature(perf-rollout): tail-risk monitoring, CI gates, and safe optimization rollout","description":"Background:\nEven correct optimization work can become dangerous if the project lacks a shared artifact contract, tail-risk monitors, or a go/no-go policy for broader adoption. The rollout layer converts one-off measurements into an operational envelope that future contributors and operators can trust.\n\nGoal:\nCreate the evidence ledger, drift/tail-risk monitors, scorecards, and rollout drills needed to promote performance changes safely.","design":"Feature scope:
- Define a unified artifact contract for baselines, profiles, replays, gauntlets, and validation-matrix outputs.
- Monitor tail regressions and regime shifts instead of only average performance.
- Define promotion gates and scorecards for accepting optimization changes, including validation completeness and logging quality.
- Script shadow, canary, fallback, and rollback drills for performance-sensitive lanes.

Intent:
Optimization work should graduate through evidence, not charisma.","acceptance_criteria":"1. CI and human reviewers consume the same artifact and validation contract.
2. Tail-latency and drift regressions become visible and actionable before broader enablement.
3. The project gains a repeatable way to decide when an optimization is safe enough to keep.
4. Promotion decisions explicitly consider whether required unit/property/integration/E2E/replay evidence and detailed logs are present and trustworthy.","notes":"This feature is what keeps the rest of the program from collapsing into a pile of benchmark screenshots with no operational meaning.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:46:23.333975250Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T19:05:03.352349280Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact-coding","ci","extreme-software-optimization","ftui-performance-control-plane","performance","performance-rollout","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-cpwfc","depends_on_id":"bd-cn7eq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:58:39.333227096Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-cpwfc","depends_on_id":"bd-lilcl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:58:42.333917535Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-cpwfc","depends_on_id":"bd-rw97d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:58:33.310200251Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-cpwfc","depends_on_id":"bd-zzfhe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:58:36.320306421Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-cs4zu","title":"test(ftui-extras): improve forms.rs test coverage for form fields, validation, and event handling","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LilacPuma","created_at":"2026-02-10T21:38:28.900560786Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T21:46:38.035442585Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T21:46:38.035412799Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["test"]}
 {"id":"bd-cu54l","title":"task(runtime-perf): derive queueing envelope and admission/coalescing policy from measured behavior","description":"Background:\nOnce runtime metrics exist, the next step is not to guess at a scheduler tweak. It is to decide what the runtime should do when load rises: which work is worth admitting, which can be delayed or coalesced, and what stable thresholds separate healthy behavior from overload.\n\nGoal:\nDefine the runtime control policy that converts measured load into explicit scheduling decisions.","design":"Work packages:
- Use measured queue and latency behavior plus the user-visible degraded-mode contract to identify stable operating regions, overload regions, and dangerous oscillation risks.
- Define admission, coalescing, or backpressure rules in human-readable terms future maintainers can understand.
- Specify conservative defaults and fallback behavior when measurements are ambiguous or the system is outside the calibrated envelope.
- Identify which work classes are allowed to degrade and which must stay strict for correctness or operator clarity.
- Explain how the resulting policy should be verified before implementation, including which focused tests and stress scripts should prove the policy is behaving as designed.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The bead states a concrete control-policy design instead of vague "make scheduling smarter" language.
2. Overload handling is explicit, bounded, and reviewable.
3. The policy is framed so implementers can later build it one lever at a time.
4. The policy is explicitly derived from both measured behavior and the earlier user-visible degraded-mode contract, so control rules do not drift away from user experience goals.","notes":"This bead is where queueing and control theory become an operational policy rather than abstract math.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:46:31.268383060Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T19:16:15.692300837Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact-coding","control","extreme-software-optimization","ftui-performance-control-plane","ftui-runtime","performance","queueing","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-cu54l","depends_on_id":"bd-4flji","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:59:48.536407027Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-cu54l","depends_on_id":"bd-dyr5a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:59:51.514285286Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -2533,7 +2533,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.7","title":"Process: Extreme Optimization loop for FrankenTerm and ftui-web","description":"Goal
- Make the Extreme Software Optimization loop mandatory across FrankenTerm + ftui-web (core engine, web renderer, native IO).

Mandatory Loop (every perf change)
1. BASELINE: capture p50/p95/p99 + memory; record run_id + git sha
2. PROFILE: CPU + allocations + (web) GPU timing when available
3. OPPORTUNITY MATRIX: Score = Impact x Confidence / Effort; implement only Score >= 2.0
4. IMPLEMENT: exactly one lever per commit; no behavior changes mixed in
5. PROVE: golden outputs + explicit isomorphism proof (ordering/ties/FP/RNG)
6. VERIFY: trace replay + sha256/checksum gates
7. REPEAT: re-profile (hotspots shift)

Deliverables
- Repeatable harnesses
  - Core parser/grid: `cargo bench -p frankenterm-core` + realistic byte-stream corpus
  - Web renderer: in-browser harness that emits machine-readable JSON (frame_time histogram, time_to_first_frame, steady-state memory)
  - Native backend: PTY harness + present-time metrics + syscall profile (e.g. `strace -c`)
- Golden artifact strategy
  - JSONL traces (reuse/extend `tests/e2e/lib/e2e_jsonl_schema.json`)
  - Frame/patch checksums stored in an append-only registry
- Templates
  - Opportunity matrix table + scoring rubric
  - Isomorphism proof template for every perf-related change
  - Regression threshold policy (when CI should fail)

Acceptance Criteria
- For each subsystem (core/web/native) we can run: baseline -> profile -> replay -> gate.
- Every optimization PR includes before/after numbers + a single-lever diff + isomorphism proof + golden verification.
","notes":"Drafted docs/profiling/extreme-optimization-loop.md (baseline/profile/opportunity matrix/one-lever/isomorphism proof/regression thresholds + harness sketches) and linked it from docs/operational-playbook.md.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkMill","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:49:57.563856458Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:40:57.929861284Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T18:40:57.929838291Z","close_reason":"Completed: added extreme optimization loop playbook + operational-playbook link","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.7","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.8","title":"Spec: Correctness strategy for terminal engines (invariants, fuzz, golden traces)","description":"Goal
- Specify correctness guarantees strong enough to justify replacing BOTH Crossterm and xterm.js.

Correctness Model
- Treat the terminal engine as a deterministic state machine driven only by explicit inputs:
  - output byte streams (VT/ANSI) and/or patches
  - input events (keyboard/mouse/touch/IME)
  - resize events
  - ticks/time (explicitly injected; never implicit global time)

Must Include
- Formal-ish invariants (written down, then enforced):
  - parser state machine safety (no panic, bounded memory growth, valid transitions)
  - grid mutations (cursor bounds, width invariants, no invalid placeholder cells)
  - scrollback correctness (capacity, wrap/reflow policy, viewport invariants)
  - selection semantics (spans scrollback+viewport; stable under resize where specified)
  - hyperlink semantics (OSC 8 lifetime + mapping)
- Proptest strategy
  - generators for byte streams, events, and resize storms
  - shrinking strategy that yields minimal repros
- Fuzzing strategy
  - entrypoints + corpora + crash triage protocol
  - every crash becomes a regression fixture
- Golden artifacts
  - conformance fixtures (byte stream -> expected grid snapshots)
  - golden trace corpus (event log -> expected checksums)
  - renderer goldens (framebuffer/patch checksums for key scenes)
- Differential testing (high-value)
  - compare FrankenTerm state against reference emulators for the same byte streams

E2E & Logging Requirements
- Native: `tests/e2e/scripts/*` must remain green and emit detailed JSONL.
- Web: a trace runner must replay the same golden traces and verify checksums.
- JSONL must conform to `tests/e2e/lib/e2e_jsonl_schema.json` (extend schema if needed, never break silently).
- Every run logs: run_id, build_id/git_sha, seed, size, caps/profile, perf counters, and checksums.

Acceptance Criteria
- A concrete test matrix mapping each user-facing feature to unit/property/fuzz/e2e coverage.
- CI hooks exist for: wasm build checks, proptest, fuzz smoke, trace replay, and checksum gates.
- A "never regress" list exists (copy/paste, selection, OSC8, resize, IME/composition, focus/mouse modes) with golden artifacts.
","notes":"Drafted docs/spec/frankenterm-correctness.md (invariants/proptest/fuzz/goldens + coverage matrix + CI hook checklist) and linked it from README Key Docs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkMill","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:04.113466063Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:34:30.644299453Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T18:34:30.644277773Z","close_reason":"Completed: added docs/spec/frankenterm-correctness.md + README link","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.8","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.9","title":"ADR: terminal backend strategy (replace Crossterm; enable WASM; unify interfaces)","description":"Goal
- Decide and document the backend architecture that removes Crossterm, supports WASM, and keeps ftui APIs clean.

Questions to Answer
- What is the core backend trait surface?
- How do we handle feature toggles (mouse/paste/focus/kitty) without leaking platform details?
- How do we represent time, sleep/ticks, and async tasks in WASM (no threads)?
- Migration plan that avoids long-lived compatibility shims.

Acceptance Criteria
- A crisp, implementable interface + crate map.
- A staged migration plan with "delete old code" checkpoints (file deletion rule applies).
","notes":"Drafted ADR in docs/adr/ADR-008-terminal-backend-strategy.md (Status: PROPOSED). Covers: backend boundary at runtime (EventSource+Presenter+Clock/Executor), new crates (ftui-backend/ftui-tty/ftui-web), isolating Crossterm during migration, wasm no-threads effect execution, staged migration with delete checkpoints + explicit-permission note for deletions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CyanTower","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:10.491519002Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:34:56.057130527Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T18:34:56.057111191Z","close_reason":"Completed: ADR drafted in docs/adr/ADR-008-terminal-backend-strategy.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.9","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-lilcl","title":"task(perf-rollout): script shadow, canary, fallback, and rollback drills for performance-sensitive changes","description":"Background:\nA sound optimization program needs operational drills just as much as it needs benchmarks. When a performance change causes trouble, the team should know how to compare old and new behavior, canary the change, fall back, roll back, and preserve enough evidence to learn from the incident.\n\nGoal:\nCreate the rollout-drill suite that operationalizes the performance evidence program.","design":"Work packages:\n- Define the drill scenarios for shadow comparison, narrow canarying, fallback, rollback, and recovery after a failed optimization.\n- Require each drill to emit structured evidence and clear operator guidance.\n- Explain how these drills connect back to the evidence ledger and scorecard.\n- Make sure the drills exercise real failure paths rather than only the clean success story.\n- Capture what future operators should do when artifacts disagree or when tail monitors trip unexpectedly.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The project gains executable operational drills rather than aspirational prose.
2. The bead links rollout actions to the artifact contract and go/no-go policy cleanly.
3. Future self can understand not just what to do, but why each drill exists and what risk it is controlling.
4. The drill suite is incomplete without scripted end-to-end runs, operator-readable summaries, and detailed machine-readable logs/artifacts for shadow, canary, fallback, rollback, and recovery paths.","notes":"Planning correction: these drills need to be treated as first-class E2E scripts with rich logs, not checklist items, so rollout safety can actually be verified.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:46:38.224201645Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T19:20:26.614066594Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact-coding","ci","extreme-software-optimization","ftui-performance-control-plane","performance","performance-rollout","rollback","shadow-run","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lilcl","depends_on_id":"bd-cn7eq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:01:31.021110347Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lilcl","depends_on_id":"bd-rw97d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:01:24.799743881Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lilcl","depends_on_id":"bd-zzfhe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:01:27.998690538Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-lrito","title":"Update all dependencies to latest stable versions","description":"## Dependency Updates Required

Closed Dependabot PRs:
- actions/setup-python 5 -> 6 (CI: Python setup action)
- codecov/codecov-action 4 -> 5 (CI: coverage upload)
- nix 0.29.0 -> 0.31.1 (MODERATE: Unix API bindings, minor version but nix crate often has breaking changes)

## Steps
1. Run cargo update
2. Review nix 0.29 -> 0.31 changelog for breaking changes (nix crate frequently removes deprecated APIs)
3. Fix any compilation errors
4. Run: cargo check --all-targets && cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
5. Run: cargo test
6. Update GH Actions versions in .github/workflows/*.yml

## Testing
- cargo test must pass
- cargo clippy must pass with -D warnings
- Verify terminal handling still works (nix crate provides ioctl, termios, etc.)
- CI must pass on push","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SunnyMarsh","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:20:41.628925003Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T04:17:24.232709235Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T04:17:24.232689528Z","close_reason":"CI already uses setup-python@v6 and codecov-action@v5; ftui-tty nix pinned to 0.31.1","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lrito","depends_on_id":"bd-ll6nr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":977,"issue_id":"bd-lrito","author":"SunnyMarsh","text":"Reopened: CI pins still on setup-python@v5 and codecov-action@v4; need bump + re-run gates","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:40:28Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-lu69j","title":"task(runtime-perf): build non-interference gauntlets for load, cancellation, shutdown, and terminal safety","description":"Background:\nRuntime performance work is only a success if the user-facing semantics stay intact, especially around cancellation, shutdown, and terminal ownership. The project needs a dedicated gauntlet that proves performance work does not regress those properties under pressure.\n\nGoal:\nCreate the runtime validation suite that enforces non-interference across the most failure-prone lifecycle edges.","design":"Work packages:
- Define gauntlet scenarios for load spikes, cancellation races, shutdown task draining, subscription churn, terminal-safety interactions, and adversarial challenge cases where runtime control policy is most likely to behave badly.
- Require focused unit/property tests for governor policy, hysteresis boundaries, and recovery semantics before E2E load scripts run.
- Require detailed logs and replay artifacts for failures so the team can tell whether the regression is semantic, scheduling, observability-related, degraded-mode UX related, or challenge-fixture fragility.
- Capture both correctness verdicts and performance metrics under the same scenarios.
- Explain what must remain invariant versus what can intentionally differ under a new control policy.
- Make the suite durable enough to guard future runtime changes beyond the first implementation wave.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Runtime optimization work gains a standing non-interference safety net with unit/property, integration, and scripted E2E stress coverage.
2. The bead clearly ties performance work back to user-visible correctness, degraded-mode clarity, and operator confidence.
3. Future implementers do not need an external plan to understand why these scenarios matter or what logs must be emitted.
4. The suite explicitly includes challenge and negative-control scenarios so runtime policy wins must survive more than idealized stress cases.","notes":"This bead keeps the runtime lane honest by forcing it to prove that 'faster' did not quietly become 'weirder.'","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:46:33.146523946Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T19:25:07.957172924Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact-coding","extreme-software-optimization","ftui-performance-control-plane","ftui-runtime","performance","runtime","shutdown","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lu69j","depends_on_id":"bd-8vstx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:06:27.572523295Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lu69j","depends_on_id":"bd-d3sfy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:00:09.606796650Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lu69j","depends_on_id":"bd-js3c3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:00:12.572941858Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lu69j","depends_on_id":"bd-pou7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:06:03.480201810Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lu69j","depends_on_id":"bd-td5el","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:00:06.630157178Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

-{"id":"bd-rw97d","title":"task(perf-rollout): define the unified performance evidence ledger and artifact contract","description":"Background:\nBy the time multiple optimization lanes are active, the project needs one place where baseline IDs, hotspot IDs, proof artifacts, replay commands, gauntlet runs, and rollout verdicts can be joined coherently. Without that, the optimization program will generate evidence faster than humans can understand it.\n\nGoal:\nCreate the shared artifact contract and evidence ledger that all performance lanes feed into and all rollout decisions consume.","design":"Work packages:
- Define the canonical identifiers and file/report schema linking workflow inventory, baselines, profiles, proofs, implementations, gauntlets, rollout decisions, and validation-matrix outputs.
- Reuse and extend the existing artifact-manifest, retention, redaction, and failure-signature planning beads rather than inventing a second artifact vocabulary.
- Explain how render, runtime, and doctor artifacts map into the same ledger without losing lane-specific detail.
- Specify required versus optional fields so missing evidence becomes a visible defect.
- Include explicit schema for detailed logs: stable IDs, reason codes, mismatch classes, artifact references, and replay commands.
- Record how humans and CI should navigate from a failed verdict to the relevant artifacts and replay commands.
- Treat this bead as an early information-architecture contract: it should be definable before every artifact-producing implementation bead is complete, while still being rich enough to absorb their outputs later without churn.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Every major artifact produced by the optimization program has a defined place in a unified ledger.
2. Missing or malformed evidence becomes easy to detect and reason about.
3. Future scorecards and rollout drills can rely on this bead directly rather than inventing their own artifact vocabulary.
4. The ledger is rich enough to explain test coverage completeness and detailed failure logging, not just pass/fail outcomes.","notes":"Planning correction: the ledger contract should be defined early enough to shape artifact-producing work, not only after all baselines and hotspots already exist. That makes later lane outputs more consistent and reduces schema drift.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:46:36.307538392Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T19:16:28.429221858Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact-coding","artifacts","ci","extreme-software-optimization","ftui-performance-control-plane","performance","performance-rollout","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-rw97d","depends_on_id":"bd-17ar5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:09:13.341300536Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-rw97d","depends_on_id":"bd-2tg0i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:08:52.875462174Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-rw97d","depends_on_id":"bd-9v2se","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:14:49.417674613Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-rw97d","depends_on_id":"bd-js3c3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:00:54.678020321Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-rw97d","depends_on_id":"bd-pou7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:06:09.496835497Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-rw97d","depends_on_id":"bd-xl1c4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:09:25.949626063Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-sfrv","title":"Runtime event parity + inline correctness hardening","description":"Harden the runtime and event pipeline so inline mode behaves predictably and event semantics are consistent across input paths.

Scope:
- Tick scheduling must deliver deterministic model updates with explicit semantics and no busy-spin.
- Inline mode must use UI-height frame sizing and consistent buffers.
- Cursor and scroll-region behavior must prevent log writes from overwriting UI.
- Input parser vs runtime event path parity for kitty keyboard, BackTab or Null or Backspace, mouse drag or scroll, and modifiers.

Deliverables:
- Precise runtime semantics documented in code and tests.
- Comprehensive unit tests and E2E scripts (PTY capture, JSON summary, reproduction commands, durations).
- No regressions in alt-screen or existing harness behavior.

Acceptance Criteria:
- All dependent tasks complete and tests pass.
- E2E logs include enough detail to debug failures quickly.
- Runtime behavior is deterministic and documented.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-02T20:28:14.983185628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T20:49:20.079177401Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T20:49:20.079153837Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-sfrv","depends_on_id":"bd-1nz8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-sfrv","depends_on_id":"bd-20vg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-sfrv","depends_on_id":"bd-3ufh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-sfrv","depends_on_id":"bd-xh8s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
